title: start with the tutor notes
description: AI tutoring becomes useful when tutor notes, learner goals, session evidence, and one next action stay connected.
tldr: AI tutoring becomes useful when a session starts with tutor notes, adds the learner’s goal and evidence, and ends with one defined next action. A generic chatbot creates another untracked conversation.

sections.h2: why should an AI tutoring session start with tutor notes?
body: Tutor notes give the session a starting point. They anchor the conversation in what the tutor already knows before the AI responds.
That context helps the session stay connected to the learner’s work instead of becoming another untracked conversation.

sections.h2: what should the session add after the tutor notes?
body: Add the learner’s goal and the evidence from the session. The goal shows what the learner is trying to do, while the evidence shows what actually appeared in the session.
Together, they give the tutoring conversation a clear reference point.

sections.h2: how does an AI tutoring session become useful?
body: End by writing one defined next action. A specific next action gives the session somewhere to go after the conversation ends.
That sequence is what makes AI tutoring useful: tutor notes, the learner’s goal, session evidence, and one defined next action.

faqs.q: How can AI tutoring become useful?
faqs.a: Start with tutor notes, add the learner’s goal and the evidence from the session, then end with one defined next action. This gives the conversation a clear structure.

faqs.q: What should an AI tutoring session include?
faqs.a: It should include tutor notes, the learner’s goal, evidence from the session, and one defined next action.

faqs.q: Why does a generic chatbot conversation feel untracked?
faqs.a: A generic chatbot creates another conversation without the structure provided by tutor notes, a learner goal, session evidence, and a defined next action.
